reason: onAbort must call cleanup() before kill/reject so the abort listener is removed and timer cleared, preventing double-reject from the close handler.
diff_snapshot:
diff --git a/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts b/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts
index 5af9e277d..9e38a6b2f 100644
--- a/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts
+++ b/packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts
@@ -78,6 +78,7 @@ function execSQL<T>(
 
     const onAbort = signal
       ? () => {
+          cleanup()
           proc.kill()
           reject(new DuckDBError("DuckDB query aborted via signal"))
         }
